body.title: Fix hollow retry contract: Vetinari alarm escalation retry-on-failure is dead on the default (production) channel-post path
body.intention: I intend that a FAILED alarm escalation leaves the alarm un-notified so the next sweep retries — on the DEFAULT (production) channel-post path, not only when a raising channel_post_fn is injected.
body.description:
**Hollow-proof found by advisor review of T-vetinari-owns-alarm-escalation.** Production constructs `VetinariDevice()` (shim.py:157) with NO channel_post_fn, so `self._channel_post` = `_default_channel_post`, which SWALLOWS exceptions and returns None (never raises). Therefore `_escalate_alarm`'s try/except catches nothing → always returns True → notify_new_alarms always stamps `mark_notified`. The retry-on-failure completion criterion of the parent ticket is dead in production; its passing tests only exercise an INJECTED raising channel_post_fn (a config production never uses).

**Fix (ripple-free):**
- `_default_channel_post(message) -> bool`: return True on success, False in the except (still logs the warning, still swallows — no raise, so `_escalate_to_akien` which ignores the return is unaffected).
- `_escalate_alarm`: `posted = self._channel_post(summary)`; treat `posted is False` as an explicit failure → log + return False (do NOT audit-log/stamp). Any non-False (None from an injecting caller that returns nothing on success, or True) counts as success → audit-log + return True. This preserves the happy-path (spy returns None) AND makes the default path able to signal failure.

**Completion criteria:** with the DEFAULT construction (`VetinariDevice()`, no injected fn) and `unseen_university.channel.post_to_channel` patched to raise, a sweep of one open alarm leaves it un-notified (notified_at is None) so a later sweep retries. This test must use the default path — NOT an injected raising fn.

**Test plan (real red->green):** new test `test_default_path_failed_post_leaves_alarm_unnotified` in tests/test_vetinari_alarm_escalation.py: patch `unseen_university.channel.post_to_channel` to raise; build `VetinariDevice()` (no channel_post_fn); seed an alarm; sweep; assert `sa.get_alarm(sig)['notified_at'] is None` and sweep returned 0. RED on the current code (default swallows → stamped → notified_at set); GREEN after the fix. Keep the existing 6 tests green (happy-path spy returns None must still stamp).

**Validation (runtime):** a real channel outage during a Vetinari sweep leaves the alarm un-stamped; the next ~5min sweep re-attempts the escalation (visible as a repeated `VetinariDevice: escalated system alarm` attempt in the vetinari INFO feed once the channel recovers, rather than a single silently-lost post).

**Affected files:** unseen_university/devices/vetinari/device.py (`_default_channel_post` + `_escalate_alarm`); tests/test_vetinari_alarm_escalation.py (new default-path test).

**Logging:** `_default_channel_post` keeps its WARNING on failure; `_escalate_alarm` logs WARNING when posted is False.

**Observable via:** log.warning('VetinariDevice: channel post failed') on the default path AND the alarm's notified_at staying null across the failed sweep.

**Rollback:** `git revert <hash>` — the change is two method bodies; reverting restores the (buggy) always-stamp behavior.

**Scope boundary:** the retry-signal contract on the default path only; no change to the sweep hook, the notifier, or the escalate-once/dedup behavior (already proven).

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)

[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
body.result: _default_channel_post returns bool (False on swallowed failure); _escalate_alarm treats 'posted is False' as failure so mark_notified is only stamped on a real post — retry-on-failure now holds on the DEFAULT (production) path, not just with an injected raising fn. Non-False (incl. None from injecting callers) still counts as success, preserving the happy path. Proven red->green on the default config; all 7 escalation tests green.
